title: Cadaver Asada
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Cadaver Asada}} __NOTOC__ {{Background|file=TilesBg.png}}{{Background|file=Void.png|opacity=0.9}} {{CSS|content= .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.69); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.69); --theme-page-text-color: #FFFFFF; --theme-border-color: #FFFFFF; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: #000000; --theme-community-header-color: #FFFFFF; } .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(10, 10, 10,0.69); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgba(10, 10, 10, 0.69); --theme-page-text-color: #FFFFFF; --theme-border-color: #FFFFFF; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: #000000; --theme-community-header-color: #FFFFFF; } ul { list-style-type: none !important; } }} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Economica:ital,wght@0,400;0,700;1,400;1,700&display=swap'); @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Work+Sans:ital,wght@0,400;0,500;0,600;1,400;1,500&display=swap'); @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Courier+Prime'); /* Font */ html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: Work Sans}}{{CSS|content= }} {{CSS|content= .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-link-color: #999; } .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-link-color: #999; } }} {{CSS|content= /* Page */ .page { margin-top: 12px; position: relative; border: solid 2px #FFF; border-radius: 5px; box-shadow: 0px 0px 20.69px 0 #FFF; /* nice 😏 */ } }} {{Warning|content=cannibalism — it's a [[partygoer]] recipe! Remember, ''gaudium est libertas''}} Haiiii {{USERNAME}}, I'm Melody Belle Evenstar, better known as the Fleshmonger and one of [[Caius Taggert]]'s [[The Co-Hosts|Co-Host]] entourage. When I heard of this ultimate cookbook thing, I thought "no way they're gonna make a manifesto of recipes without inviting me", so... WELCOME BACK TO COOKIN' WITH MELODY! This is a dish called "'''Cadaver Asada'''", one of my personal favourites, and is featured as a tool in a poison-based murder in Caius's recent original musical, ''Fiddlesticks!''Amazing show. I don't blame you if you haven't watched it though, especially since it only shows in [[Caius Taggert|Caius]]'s "Fjord Theatre", which is modelled after that place where Lincoln died. I mean, it's ''supposed'' to be modelled after that, but Caius either doesn't know or doesn't care that Festus is a mechanical engineer and not an architect. {{Notice|body=Pls no autocannibalism! I may be crazy, but I'm not ''that'' crazy.}} == Ingredients!!! == Let's cut to the chase. Here's the ingredients list: * 1kg of human/[[Phenomenon 35|wretch]]/[[Entity 9|Facelings|faceling]]/[[partygoer]] meat from the general abdominal area of the body. Preferably taken from the corpse of a dead medical patient — it's called ''cadaver'' for a reason. I suggest killing the victim yourself.FYI: Euphoria has way too many medical records with "Time of Death: ''DINNER TIME''" scrawled on them. * 10g of kosher kidney stones.In case it wasn't clear, these need to be taken from the body of an observant Jew. * 15g of refrigerated pancreas shavings. * 1/3 cup of fresh bile. * 1/2 cup of fresh blood. * 1/4 cup of vitreous humour. * 4 thyroid glands, minced. * 1 teaspoon of stomach acid, preferably from the main cadaver the meat came from. * 6-7 European medicinal leeches (''H. medicinalis'').They won't be ''in'' the final dish, if that's what you were thinking, idiots. Do you leave the alcohol in the bread after putting the yeast in? == Instructions!!! == INSTRUCTIONS!!! # '''Making marinade:''' In a large bowl, whisk together the 10g of kosher kidney stones, 15g of refrigerated pancreas shavings, 1/3 cup of fresh bile, 1/2 cup of fresh blood, 1/4 cup of vitreous humour, and the 4 thyroid glands, until well combined. This is the marinade. # '''De-ensanguining the meat:''' Stick the 6-7 leeches in the meat until blood is drained completely, which should take around 1 hour. # '''Marinating the meat:''' Remove the leechesPlease don't forget to do this. and place the de-ensanguined meat in the marinade, turning it to ensure both sides are fully coated. Refrigerate for ideally 2 to 4 hours. # '''Preheating a grill:''' When ready to cook, preheat an outdoor grill to medium-high heat, preferably in a desolate outdoor level where you won't get jumped like [[Level 11]]. Brush the grill grates lightly with a teaspoon of stomach acid. # '''Grilling the meat:''' Remove the meat from the marinade and let it come to room temperature while the grill heats up. Place the steak on the grill and cook for 6 to 7 minutes per side. # '''Resting:''' No, you're not resting, the meat is. Transfer it to a cutting board and allow it to rest for 5 minutes before slicing into pieces. # '''Seasoning (optional):''' Add flakes of palm-skin or kidney stones for seasoning. And with those six to seven simple steps, you should have some edible '''Cadaver Asada''' ready to eat. ''Bon cannibalisme! '' == Excerpt from ''Fiddlesticks!'' == {{Notice|body=This contains spoilers for ''Fiddlesticks!'' Y'all know I hate spoilers but I kinda have to include a tale in here; my lawyer (a.k.a. Bacchus) told me that this would be the best choice, and that nobody is as spoilerphobic as I am.Says the literal haemophobic [[partygoer]] >:)}} This scene dictates the coming of a messenger (Nuntius) from the main antagonist (Megeus) to the house of the two leproticHonestly, this detail is kinda discriminatory. I guess [[Caius Taggert|Caius]] hasn't really got out of that eighteenth century mindset yet. protagonists (Celious & Deacon), in an attempt to peacefully negotiate a truce between their two armies. They sit down to have a feast, of course with a little bit of poison.
'''ACT II,''' SCENE II
The house of '''CELIOUS''' and '''DEACON'''. ''A knock from the door.'' '''CELIOUS:'''
What ho, Deacon! There is a visitor at our door. '''DEACON:'''
Then fetch thou him. Am I thy servant, Celious? '''CELIOUS:'''
But Celious, Deacon, is neither thy servant. No matter, I fetch the visitor at thy behest, friend. '''''CELIOUS''' opens the door. Enter '''NUNTIUS'''.'' '''CELIOUS:'''
Why, 'tis a messenger, and by his breast emblazoned is the mark of Megeus! '''NUNTIUS:'''
Hearken, masters! I come to speak as my sire's mouth unto you, so that we may of an armistice parley. '''DEACON:'''
An armistice, saist thou? Youth, what is thy name? '''NUNTIUS:'''
Nuntius is my name, good sir. '''DEACON:'''
Then know thou, humble Nuntius, this: In this house we parley not ere we feast. Celious, get ready the viands! '''CELIOUS:'''
Celious, Deacon, is not thy servant. '''DEACON:'''
Very well, I then myself go to prepare a dish for this houseguest. ''Exit '''DEACON'''.'' '''NUNTIUS:'''
This, indeed, is most unexpect'd, good master Celious. Blest art thou for thy hospitality, and also master Deacon. But I prithee, what feast us upon this fine day? '''CELIOUS:'''
Today, youth, Deacon maketh for thee the delicacy of the forsook Jovial Ones who dwelleth in the Realm Beyond, the Cadaver Asada. '''NUNTIUS:'''
And, pray, which is that? '''CELIOUS:'''
Why, 'tis a celebrated fare in our country and lands around; the broilèd flesh, that is, of man, rested in blood and bile and the other humours that its juices are ripe. '''NUNTIUS:'''
But, sir, Nuntius cannot the flesh of man eat! I come from a county, Lord Megeus's land, where to devour thy fellow people is a heinous sin! '''CELIOUS:'''
But we, we are not thy kinsmen, Nuntius. The county of Megeus and our own fair kingdom are far apart as the crow flieth; ne'er shall the twain meet — and so ne'er shall our twain peoples become one. To eat the Cadaver Asada, in thine eyne, should not be to devour thy fellows in sin but merely to eat meat of an alien race which thy countrymen are not. Ah, here cometh Deacon, with the dish. ''Enter '''DEACON'''. He carries three dishes of a rich-looking meat, and sets them upon the table.'' '''DEACON:'''
Feast, companions! Let not the throes of hunger imprison you for a bit longer. '''CELIOUS:'''
Feast, Nuntius! Wherefore art thou sadden'd so? '''NUNTIUS:'''
I cannot eat another man! I cannot and wonnot! This meat, this that lieth before me, is a son of Adam, else a daughter of Eve, and I cannot and wonnot eat it! Heard ye not of the Romans' old stories of those which were cannibals, which were punish'd soundly by the gods? Prithee, I shall do aught, but eat this I wonnot! '''DEACON:'''
What, Nuntius! The Romans' yarns are but wives' tales of old— '''CELIOUS:'''
Peace, ye fat guts! Youth, devour'st thou not a son nor daughter of Eve; for 'tis said that in the days of yore our forefathers and grandfathers were but the offspring of malefic Lilith, who came ere Eve; and 'tis also said that our countrymen long ago gave our lives to the god Belzebuth in place of He who ye barbarian men of Megeus worship. Lo, we are not men as thou art at all, one could say. '''NUNTIUS:'''
Well, if this foul act may yet forge decades of peace betwixt the county of Lord Megeus and this frightening land, mayhap then 'twill be... worth the sin. Forgive Thou poor Nuntius, Lord! I do only what best I see for my country! '''''NUNTIUS''' begins to eat.'' '''DEACON:'''
What of the Cadaver Asada, Nuntius? '''NUNTIUS:'''
'Tis... '''DEACON:'''
Ah, I know, youth, I know. 'Tis an acquired taste. Shan't thou straightway appreciate its daintiness. '''NUNTIUS:'''
I... still do not feel a full man committing such a deed. '''DEACON:'''
It matters not, Nuntius, it matters not... After all, hast thou a greater worry to plague thy mind at present than the humanly morality of cannibalism. '''CELIOUS:'''
A greater worry, indeed? O Deacon, what hast put thou in poor Nuntius's victuals? '''NUNTIUS:'''
He hath something put in my dish? '''DEACON:'''
Peace, youth! I have not much put in thy dish, but the juices of curst hebenon. '''CELIOUS:'''
Why, but hebenon is a poison! Art thou quite mad, Deacon? Thou hast murder'd another houseguest yet! '''NUNTIUS:'''
Murder'd? O Hell, O spite, O gadzooks and zounds: am I to die? '''DEACON:'''
Peace, peace! I vouchsafe no trust in whomsoever dread Megeus sendeth unto our doorstep; they must be with a wanion manag'd! An illusive fiend he is, that lord. '''NUNTIUS:'''
Fiddlesticks, I am dead! — Then fall, Nuntius! '''''NUNTIUS''' falls to the ground dramatically, dead.'' '''CELIOUS:'''
Pray, Deacon, cease to kill our visitors such! 'Tis nowise a courteous bearing. '''DEACON:'''
Am I thy servant, Celious? I slay whom I wish. '''CELIOUS:'''
''(sigh)''
And the cadaver? '''DEACON:'''
To be our next meal, then. Come, Celious, we have work. ''Exeunt '''CELIOUS''' and '''DEACON''', carrying the body of '''NUNTIUS'''.'' ''End of scene.''
